response.justification: While the candidate exceeds the minimum experience requirement (19+ years with a Bachelor's degree) and demonstrates strong software engineering practices, this is a fundamental domain mismatch. The Principal Performance Architect role requires specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and AI workload optimization at the hardware level. The candidate's entire career trajectory is in web, backend, mobile, and application-level performance optimization—not hardware design or systems-on-chip architecture. There is no evidence of the critical hardware-software co-design expertise needed for this principal-level role.
strengths: 19+ years of technical experience with demonstrated depth across multiple languages and systems-level programming
Strong performance optimization mindset shown through low-latency application work at Dunnhumby and microservices architecture expertise
Proven ability to navigate complex technical domains and mentor others, with prior C/C++ systems programming experience
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware design experience; entire career focused on application software rather than hardware architecture
No evidence of GPU architecture expertise, SIMD/MIMD instruction sets, or deep neural network inference optimization at the hardware level
Career progression is in web/backend/mobile software, not hardware-software co-design or cloud infrastructure hardware optimization

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP and GPU architecture expertise
No AI accelerator or hardware performance optimization background
